word_list.word: a fool may give a wise man counsel
word_list.pronunciation: …ź fňąuňźl mňąe…™ …°ňą…™v …ź wňąa…™z mňą√¶n kňąa äns…ôl

senses.definition: used to suggest that one should be discerning and evaluate any advice or suggestions received, regardless of the source

word_list.word: a word to the wise is (sufficient|enough)
word_list.pronunciation: …ź wňą…úňźd t…ô √į…ô wňąa…™z …™z s…ôfňą…™ É…ônt …™nňą Ćf

senses.definition: used to suggest that a wise person only needs a brief hint or explanation to fully understand something

examples.x: The boss didn't need to explain the consequences of being late again; a word to the wise is sufficient.

senses.definition: used to imply that giving advice is easy and does not necessarily require much effort or expertise
used to imply that those who give advice or counsel are typically not exposed to the potential dangers or negative consequences that may arise from the decisions made based on their recommendations

examples.x: The business consultant recommended that we expand our operations overseas, but when we faced unexpected challenges and losses, he was not held accountable, highlighting the reality that advisors run no risks.

word_list.word: advice is least heeded when most needed
word_list.pronunciation: …źdvňąa…™s …™z lňąiňźst hňąiňźdŠĶĽd w…õn mňąo äst nňąiňźdŠĶĽd

senses.definition: used to convey the idea that people are often less likely to take advice or guidance when they are in dire or challenging situations
used to imply that advice or suggestions are not the same as orders or commands, and that one is free to accept or reject them as they see fit

examples.x: The manager offered her team suggestions on how to complete the project, but reminded them that counsel is no command and that they were free to pursue their own strategies.

senses.definition: used to suggest that leading by example is more effective than giving instructions or preaching, as people are often more influenced by actions than words

word_list.word: it is a silly goose that comes to a fox's sermon
word_list.pronunciation: …™t …™z …ź sňą…™li …°ňąuňźs √į√¶t kňą Ćmz t ä …ź fňą…Ďňźks…™z sňą…úňźm…ôn

senses.definition: used to advise individuals to be cautious and discerning in choosing whom they trust and follow for advice or guidance
